gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A single `on <ErrorClass> => { … }` rule. Presentation/routing only (I4); a `None` field leaves"] # [doc = " the corresponding default in place."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq , Serialize , Deserialize)] pub struct Rule { # [doc = " A presentation message override (does not change the error's identity or reason)."] # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] pub message : Option < String > , # [doc = " Free-form string tags (v0; §4.4 DN04-Q2)."] # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "BTreeSet::is_empty")] pub tags : BTreeSet < String > , # [doc = " The default verbosity for diagnostics under this rule."] # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] pub level : Option < Level > , # [doc = " An output route — *where* the presentation goes, never *whether* the error propagates (I1)."] # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] pub route : Option < String > , }

gaps.snippet: impl Rule { # [doc = " An empty rule (all defaults)."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { Rule :: default () } # [doc = " Set the presentation message."] # [must_use] pub fn message (mut self , m : impl Into < String >) -> Self { self . message = Some (m . into ()) ; self } # [doc = " Add a tag."] # [must_use] pub fn tag (mut self , t : impl Into < String >) -> Self { self . tags . insert (t . into ()) ; self } # [doc = " Set the level."] # [must_use] pub fn level (mut self , l : Level) -> Self { self . level = Some (l) ; self } # [doc = " Set the route from a free-form string (the on-the-wire/`PolicyFile` projection form). Prefer"] # [doc = " [`route_to`](Rule::route_to) for a route in the closed v0 set; a string that does not resolve to"] # [doc = " a [`Route`](super::sink::Route) is an explicit [`UnknownRoute`](super::sink::UnknownRoute) at"] # [doc = " sink-resolution time (never a silent misroute — RFC-0013 §8)."] # [must_use] pub fn route (mut self , r : impl Into < String >) -> Self { self . route = Some (r . into ()) ; self } # [doc = " Set the route from the **closed v0** [`Route`](super::sink::Route) vocabulary (the checked path)."] # [must_use] pub fn route_to (mut self , r : super :: sink :: Route) -> Self { self . route = Some (r . as_str () . to_owned ()) ; self } }

gaps.snippet: impl DiagnosticPolicy { # [doc = " An empty policy."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { DiagnosticPolicy :: default () } # [doc = " Add a rule for `class`, **resolving the class name through the registry** (X1). A duplicate"] # [doc = " class replaces the prior rule and returns it."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`UnknownClass`] if `class` is not in `registry` — an explicit configuration error"] # [doc = " (never silently ignored, never coerced into code)."] pub fn on (& mut self , registry : & ClassRegistry , class : & str , rule : Rule ,) -> Result < Option < Rule > , UnknownClass > { let name = registry . resolve (class) ? ; Ok (self . rules . insert (name , rule)) } # [doc = " The rule for a resolved class, if any."] # [must_use] pub fn rule_for (& self , class : & ClassName) -> Option < & Rule > { self . rules . get (class) } # [doc = " The rules, in deterministic (class-sorted) order."] pub fn rules (& self) -> impl Iterator < Item = (& ClassName , & Rule) > { self . rules . iter () } # [doc = " Whether the policy has no rules."] # [must_use] pub fn is_empty (& self) -> bool { self . rules . is_empty () } # [doc = " The **content address** of this policy (RFC-0005 `PolicyRef`; ADR-006) — a deterministic"] # [doc = " BLAKE3 over its canonical, sorted rules. Diffable and identity-stable regardless of insertion"] # [doc = " order or which on-disk format (JSON/YAML/TOML) a file used (§4.7)."] # [must_use] pub fn content_id (& self) -> ContentHash { let mut h = blake3 :: Hasher :: new () ; let blob = | hasher : & mut blake3 :: Hasher , bytes : & [u8] | { hasher . update (& (bytes . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; hasher . update (bytes) ; } ; blob (& mut h , b"mycelium.diagnostic-policy.v1") ; h . update (& (self . rules . len () as u64) . to_le_bytes ()) ; for (class , rule) in & self . rules { blob (& mut h , class . as_str () . as_bytes ()) ; let json = serde_json :: to_string (rule) . expect ("a rule serializes") ; blob (& mut h , json . as_bytes ()) ; } let hex = h . finalize () . to_hex () ; ContentHash :: from_parts ("blake3" , hex . as_str ()) . expect ("blake3 hex is a valid digest") } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A serializable projection of a policy (RFC-0013 §4.7: a file is a *projection of* the canonical"] # [doc = " declaration, not the source of truth). Class names are strings here; re-ingesting validates them"] # [doc = " through the registry (X1), so an on-disk file can never smuggle in an unknown/evaluated class."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq , Serialize , Deserialize)] pub struct PolicyFile { # [doc = " `class name -> rule`."] # [serde (default)] pub on : BTreeMap < String , Rule > , }

gaps.snippet: impl DiagnosticPolicy { # [doc = " Project this policy to a serializable [`PolicyFile`] (one on-disk form; §4.7)."] # [must_use] pub fn to_file (& self) -> PolicyFile { PolicyFile { on : self . rules . iter () . map (| (c , r) | (c . as_str () . to_owned () , r . clone ())) . collect () , } } # [doc = " Ingest a [`PolicyFile`], **resolving every class name through the registry** (X1). An unknown"] # [doc = " class anywhere in the file is an explicit error — the file is rejected as a whole, never"] # [doc = " partially/silently applied."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " Returns [`UnknownClass`] for the first class name not in `registry`."] pub fn from_file (registry : & ClassRegistry , file : & PolicyFile) -> Result < Self , UnknownClass > { let mut policy = DiagnosticPolicy :: new () ; for (class , rule) in & file . on { policy . on (registry , class , rule . clone ()) ? ; } Ok (policy) } }
